prompt:
Master-data platform ticket. Our nightly partner-maintenance job writes to S/4HANA one PATCH at a time over plain HTTP and it's a mess: it re-fetches a CSRF token for every single call, half-applies related updates when one of them fails, and when the gateway rejects a stale token the job just dies at 2am. Rebuild the write path as a C# library (namespace SapBatch) that speaks the gateway's $batch channel properly: one cached CSRF session, change sets that live and die together, and per-operation result decoding. The xunit suite BatchTests.cs in this project is the acceptance contract; it, the csproj, .gitignore, and everything under docs/ are protected — add your source files to this same test project.

Research comes first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official documentation — the OData 2.0 Batch Processing specification (the exact multipart/mixed framing: what Content-Type and transfer encoding every part carries, how change sets nest, what Content-ID is for, what the spec says a change set may NOT contain, and its all-or-none rule), SAP's official Cloud SDK documentation for how SAP gateways actually do CSRF (which header, which value, what comes back in the response headers AND cookies, and why the token is cached), and the SAP Business Accelerator Hub record for API_BUSINESS_PARTNER to confirm the current service path/version and that the A2X service supports batch. First-party sources only (odata.org for the protocol, sap.github.io/cloud-sdk and api.sap.com/help.sap.com for SAP). docs/contract.json pins our mock's wire contract and docs/official_sources.json the provenance; if live docs contradict a pin, call it out in your summary and leave protected files alone. Cite the exact official URLs you actually read in your final message.

Shape the tests compile against (internal design beyond this is yours):
- BatchRequestBuilder(batchBoundary, changesetBoundary) with AddRetrieve(pathAndQuery), AddChangeset(params BatchWriteOperation[]) and Build() returning the full multipart payload — the tests compare it byte for byte, so the line discipline, part headers, and terminators must match what the spec mandates. BatchWriteOperation.Post(path, jsonBody, contentId) and .Merge(path, jsonBody, contentId); duplicate Content-IDs within one change set are an ArgumentException naming Content-ID. Reads and writes must live where the spec says they live — one of them is banned inside change sets, and the builder's API should make the legal structure the only expressible one.
- BatchGateway(HttpClient, serviceRootUrl) with Task<BatchResponse> SubmitAsync(builder): performs the documented token pre-flight against the service root (mind what SAP sends the token back in — there are two carriers and the write needs both), caches it across submissions, POSTs the batch with the boundary-parameterized content type, and on the documented token-rejection response refreshes exactly once and retries the SAME payload once — a second rejection throws SapCsrfException whose message never contains a token value.
- BatchResponse.Parse(contentTypeHeader, body) (also used by SubmitAsync): walks the multipart response, yielding Retrieves (RetrievePartResult: Status, Body) and Changesets (ChangesetResult: Succeeded, Operations keyed by Content-ID with Status/Body/Location, Error). Decode 201-with-body and 204-without-body operations. When the gateway failed a change set it answers with something structurally DIFFERENT inside that part — the spec's atomicity rule tells you what that means for every operation you attempted: report the change set failed, zero operations applied, and surface the SAP error document (code, message, and the whole innererror block preserved verbatim as RawJson).

Acceptance: dotnet test (first run restores pinned NuGet packages). Loopback mock only — no live gateway, no real tokens, no sleeps.

research.product: SAP S/4HANA — Gateway OData V2 write channel (Business Partner A2X $batch with CSRF protection)

official_sources.title: Batch Processing (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: batch POST to the $batch endpoint with Content-Type multipart/mixed and a boundary; parts require Content-Type: application/http and Content-Transfer-Encoding: binary; 'ChangeSets cannot contain retrieve requests and cannot be nested'; Content-ID referencing within a change set; 'the server must successfully process and apply all the requests in the ChangeSet or else apply none'; 202 Accepted responses mirroring the multipart structure in request order

official_sources.title: CSRF Fetching | SAP Cloud SDK (official SAP SDK docs)
official_sources.used_for: x-csrf-token header with value fetch on a HEAD pre-flight against the resource path; tokens required for non-GET requests; Set-Cookie headers of the token response must be considered for the subsequent request

official_sources.title: Use the OData v2 Type-safe Client API | SAP Cloud SDK for Java (official SAP SDK docs)
official_sources.used_for: 'the OData service sends back the CSRF token twice: once in the x-csrf-token header and once in a Set-Cookie header'; pre-flight before modification requests and manual caching/reuse of the token; 'per the specification, either all operations or none within the same changeset are successful'; per-changeset result extraction and error checking

official_sources.title: SAP Business Accelerator Hub catalog record — Business Partner (A2X), API_BUSINESS_PARTNER 1.7.0
official_sources.used_for: current API version (1.7.0), ODATA service code and service URL .../sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER, explicit batch-processing support statement for the A2X service

official_sources.title: Operations (OData Version 2.0)
official_sources.used_for: response-code conventions embedded in batch parts: 201 Created with Location for POST, 204 No Content for updates; error conditions as 4xx responses with detailed bodies

research.language_rationale: The master-data write jobs here run on .NET services; SAP documents Gateway writes as protocol-level OData 2.0 batch requests over HTTP with header-based CSRF protection, so HttpClient plus exact multipart framing per the OData 2.0 specification is the idiomatic dependency-free surface (SAP's own SDK docs describe the identical token/changeset semantics this fixture pins).
